_schema: One JSON object per line — the growth agent's structured engagement-experiment log (append-only). Fields: date (YYYY-MM-DD), rubric_lever (e.g. R1), hypothesis (one line), files (list of prompt files changed), commit (sha), predicted ({metric, dir:up|down}), baseline_note (measured baseline at ship time), status (running|measuring|promoted|reverted), verdict (null until settled). The agent skips any line containing _schema.
note: Seed line. Real experiments are appended below by the daily run.

hypothesis: Thumbnail hooks that stop echoing the title and instead open a curiosity gap (withhold the resolution, no title words) will raise CTR once impressions flow.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 9/12 golden-set hooks reused the title's distinctive words (ch2-code hook 'Um Servidor Para Tudo' a literal title slice; ch1-code '5 Lines Fix Slow RAG'). After change, title-echo ~1/12 with genuine gaps; both gated renders visible=True/no-fallback, verify_storyboard 38/38.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-04 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: measured CTR still 0 on both channels — impressions not flowing, so no CTR cohort). Re-render-judged: today's baseline golden set shows 0/4 thumbnail hooks echo the title, all genuine curiosity gaps ('One tweak changes everything' vs title 'Fix Your Slow RAG'; 'Uma conexao que substitui mil' vs 'Um Servidor MCP Para Tudo'). Change holds; kept in code. Re-settle on measured CTR once impressions populate.

hypothesis: Portrait diagram beats waste ~55% of the 9:16 frame (landscape-ish viewBox width-fit to the frame); narrowing the viewBox so the stacked flow renders larger and fills more vertical space will improve mobile clarity and reduce mid-body dead-screen that drags retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge (measured retention is the confirmed weak spot: ch1 short avp 36.4%, ch2 21.4%, avd ~10s on ~35s shorts). Baseline ch1-code 4-node diagram filled ~51% frame width / ~38% height with large empty top. After (viewBox 1000->760, node 560->600): ch1-code 3-node diagram fills ~73% width and more vertical space, nothing clipped, both gated renders visible=True/no-fallback, verify_storyboard 40/40.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-07 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: fix shipped ~12:00 UTC 07-04 and the mature cutoff is that same hour, so the mature post-fix retention cohort is ~empty — no SIGNAL-RICH verdict possible yet). Render-judged for the 3rd consecutive day: 07-07 baseline golden set shows portrait diagrams filling the frame (ch1-code 4-node stack ~62% width, ch1-concept 3-node large, nothing clipped). Change holds; kept in code. Re-check retention (avp of diagram-carrying videos) once the post-07-04 cohort matures with creation_config attribution.

hypothesis: Portrait cmp beats waste the 9:16 frame (two 42%-width side-by-side columns, small text, ~70% dead frame) and the absolutely-centered VS badge collides with card text; stacking the cards full-width with the badge in normal flow between them improves mobile clarity and cuts mid-body dead-frame that drags retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge (ch2 short avp 21.4%, ch1 35.7% — retention is the confirmed weak spot). Baseline ch2-concept/ch1-code cmp frames: narrow middle band, VS badge touching card text (ch2-concept). After: full-width stacked cards, badge in flow (collision structurally impossible), text ~2x larger, block fills ~35% of frame height; both gated renders visible=True/no-fallback; verify_storyboard 40/40.
verdict: PROMOTED 2026-07-08 (SIGNAL-SCARCE: every published video carrying creation_config is still <72h with 0 measured views, so no retention cohort). Render-judged on the 07-08 baseline: ch1-code b1_cmp and ch2-concept b5_cmp both full-width stacked cards, VS badge in flow between them, no text collision, large readable text. Change holds; kept in code. Re-check retention via creation_config beat-mix attribution once the cmp-carrying cohort matures.

hypothesis: Portrait diagram edge labels centered on the vertical connector get struck through by the line and can clip the node border above; placing them beside the line (start-anchored, gap-centered) with a paint-order stroke halo makes diagram beats legible on mobile and supports retention.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-06 baseline frames show the connector striking through 'context' (ch1-concept, label also clipping the Store box border) and 'protocolo' (ch2-code). After: ch1-concept 'store' label beside the line, centered in the gap, halo intact, nothing clipped; ch2-code diagram (unlabeled this render) unchanged-good. verify_storyboard 40/40; both gated renders visible=True, no fallback.

hypothesis: Storyboards for videos ABOUT a tool/API/protocol/technique that include a concrete code/command beat (even when the narration never reads code aloud) carry more information per second and lift retention vs text-only beat mixes.
baseline_note: Render-and-judge: 07-07 baseline ch2-code (MCP explainer) had NO code and NO stat beat (hook,lst,term,cmp,diagram,stmt,quote,cta) — R2 scored 1, the only weak lever. After rule 2b: ch2-code mix hook,stat,term,diagram,code,cmp,quote,cta with a correct @mcp.tool() snippet and grounded 1x stat; ch1-concept control gained a sensible ctx-search/llm/db.save memory-loop snippet, no lever dropped. verify_storyboard 40/40, both gated renders visible=True no fallback.

hypothesis: Pinning idea-title and script language to the channel's render-profile voice stops wrong-language videos (3 EN videos shipped to the PT channel 07-07) and lifts ch2 retention/subs on affected topics vs those EN outliers.
baseline_note: Videos 495-497 (t10, ch2): English scripts narrated by pt-BR-AntonioNeural, published 07-07. Verified synchronously: generate_ideas(language='Brazilian Portuguese') returns PT titles; _generate_script(EN title, pt-BR voice) returns PT script; gated renders ch2-code (PT) / ch1-code (EN) clean, no lever dropped; verify_storyboard 40/40; live check: POST /api/topics/10/generate produced PT draft 543 through the restarted app.
